name: Ira Sullivan
abstract: Ira Sullivan (May 1, 1931 – September 21, 2020) was an American jazz trumpeter, flugelhornist, flautist, saxophonist, and composer born in Washington, D.C., United States. An active musician since the 1950s, he often worked with Red Rodney and Lin Halliday.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Goin' to Chicago
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Ira Sullivan two years, and it was great experience
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: So Red and Ira were the house horn men so I was very close with Ira Sullivan... So we were actually in a band together then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 27, 2007 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I had a band that was made up of Larry Gray on bass... Jodie Christian on piano, and Ira Sullivan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Donald Byrd, Ira Sullivan, Kenny Drew, myself, and Art Blakey.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer states Ira Sullivan was a neighbor of the interviewer, not the interviewee. The interviewee's response 'And this is really what -- that was my first introduction to him' refers to the previous topic or the interviewer's story, not a personal connection with Sullivan.

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: Ira Sullivan, Herbie Hancock was around and he was just starting too, Jack DeJohnette was a piano player. We would play at the Student Union at Chicago every Thursday... Ira Sullivan was an amazing talent in Chicago.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012
